zKfontTools.misc.timeTools.py -- tools for working with OpenType timestamps.
print_function
division
absolute_import)
*N)	ip

Decc
	Convert a tuple or struct_time representing a time as returned by gmtime()
	or localtime() to a 24-character string of the following form:
	>>> asctime(time.gmtime(0))
	'Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970'
	If t is not provided, the current time as returned by localtime() is used.
	Locale information is not used by asctime().
	This is meant to normalise the output of the built-in time.asctime() across
	different platforms and Python versions.
	In Python 3.x, the day of the month is right-justified, whereas on Windows
	Python 2.7 it is padded with zeros.
	See https://github.com/behdad/fonttools/issues/455
%s %s %2s %sz
%H:%M:%S %Y)
time
	localtime
DAYNAMES
tm_wday

tm_mon
tm_mday
strftime)
</usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/fontTools/misc/timeTools.py
asctime
gmtime
epoch_diff
valuer
timestampToString,
calendar
timegmr
strptimer
timestampFromString/
SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH)
environ
intr
source_date_epochr
timestampNow2
timestampSinceEpoch9
__main__)

fontTools.misc.py23r

sysZ
doctest

failedr
<module>
